activation_results.reason: Input matches the description's canonical use case verbatim.
Verb swap from Generate to Produce preserves intent and target artefact.
Explicit invocation by skill name unambiguously triggers activation.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to Meta Ads copy generation.
Meta-question about skills generally is out of scope for ad copy generation.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'Australian', 'Aussie-Made', 'AU English' explicitly noted in QA; no American spellings detected.

criterion_results.text: Output is machine-readable where the description implies (valid JSON/SQL/YAML)
criterion_results.evidence: Description implies copy variants, not structured data; artefact provides structured markdown with a tabular A/B matrix which is the appropriate machine-parseable form.
Artefact includes Primary text, Headlines, Descriptions, A/B test matrix, QA summary, and Next steps sections.

judge_result.notes: Artefact demonstrates the expected output shape: multiple primary-text angles (problem, benefit, story, social proof, urgency, contrarian), headline and description variants within character limits, an A/B matrix, and a QA summary. Australian English and brand-voice constraints are honoured. Criterion 3 was truncated in the prompt (no explicit section list given), so it was interpreted as the canonical sections implied by the description (primary text, headlines, CTA/descriptions) — all present. Overall verdict marked 'partial' only because criterion 3 was incompletely specified; on the visible criteria the artefact passes.
